Enrolled course three days after session begun, I asked Prof. Cameron the deadline for assignments I missed via email, but never getting a single word back. A week later, he treats my catch-up assignments as 'regular' late work, by giving them all half credit no matter I turned them in ASAP. Where is all my effort putting in? What a waste of time!
Boring unenthusiastic lectures. Could almost ruin an interesting subject

I didn't know anything about historical archaeology before this class, but Monroe totally taught us about how important a field it is.  Great lecturer.  Tests are not too hard, he gives the questions out in advance.  Be prepared for a lot of reading.

analysis.name: Carson Schutze
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Schutze is one of the best professors I have ever had at UCLA.  If you study everything carefully on the slides and treat the homework sheets seriously, you will likely end up doing pretty well. And when you have things that you really don't understand, either email him or go to his office hours. He is always willing to help.
Horrible and boring. Had him for Syntax and thought this class, Language Processing,  would be better since this his field, but I was so wrong! The class was SO HARD and POINTLESS. Avoid if possible.

Stay away! I dropped week 6. Carson makes no effort to teach to an undergraduate, lower-div level. He has clear communication, but grades all assignments harshly. Each homework (one/week) took me HOURS to complete. He is frequently late/absent for office hours and doesn't care if anyone succeeds. I will retake with a prof who supports my success.

This was the most frustrating class I have ever taken. Its meant to be an intro to linguistics and yet he spoke to us as if we had been studying the material for the last four years. Even students who had previously taken this class at the community college level struggled to understand. He made everything WAY more complex than it needed to be.
i completely disagree- carson was wonderful, very willing to answer questions and help during office hours.

analysis.name: John Carriero
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He's a good professor and extremely nice to the point were people take advantage. He Does have a monotone voice but he does record his lectures. Yet he has a way of explaining difficult information in a more simple manner. Attendance is a must. And he does expect students to have a high commitment to the course but it isn't overbearing.

Professor Carriero is tough. He has strong material knowledge, but he doesn't always convey it in a clear manner. I found him to be more helpful in one on one office hours. He discourages class discussion or questions during lecture and I found that disheartening. All in all I'd take him again because of his keen knowledge of philosophy.

John is incredible. He made philosophers whom I had no appreciation for beforehand among my favorites, he is open to help during personal trials, he is a great professor and an exemplary person. If you are serious about furthering your philosophical education, then you will regret having not taken him.

He's amazing at getting difficult comcepts across very clearly. He's helpful, but he will not do your work for you, and demands ahrd work from his students. Do not expect to sakte in his classes! FYI: his nickname is John Aristotle, if that gives you any clue!

analysis.name: Calvin Normore
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Calvin is among my favorite professors. I had him for multiple classes and he was one of the reasons why I transferred to UCLA instead of Berkeley. He finds the profundity in "dumb" questions, he rarely offers his own philosophical sympathies in (what I take to be) an attempt to give credibility to opposing views. Character limits prevent full grat

Everyone knows Calvin is an amazing philosopher. But as a professor, he sucks. His lectures are ahrd to follow, and he is the most disoprganized man you've ever seen. He didnt answer emails, would never have his lectures ready, went off on tangents, and is not good at lectureing. If you need help, go to office hours-- hes better in that setting.

Outstanding professor. I took Phil 100B & Phil 154B with him. Extremely knowledgeable about the topics in his courses. Provides his own, creative examples. Doesn't read off of boring PowerPoint slide shows. Able to keep class interested & doesn't bore the class with monotony. Incorporates good humor into his examples. Highly recommend him!!

He is about the Best Professor any one could have. He is above all, dedicated to his students. He is not easy, but more than willing to give as much help as you need. He has integrity and he is hands down brilliant! A very fair grader. Which is important because many of the TA's are problematic, some are good.

Normore is a competent professor. However, the class I took with him, Philosophy of Mind, was not engaging. I must mention that Normore answered any questions I had and was very patient with others. I do not recommend the class, but perhaps other classes with this professor would be more interesting.

analysis.name: Christopher Mott
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I took two of Professor Mott's classes, and was thoroughly engaged. I am an AP English Language and Literature teacher now and use so many of his strategies still. How great of a professor is he? He had a full house the week of the Northridge earthquake and we didn't skip a beat during an aftershock. Okay maybe I screamed, but we kept at it still!

I used to hate american novels before i took this class, but now i love them! Mott is hilarious, so his lecture are interesting, but he can sometimes be hard to understand because he doesn't really tell you what he's explaining. but that's ok because you don't REALLY have to go to lecture to do well in the class. just read and know all the novels!
Really caring, a gentleman, and rather good looking. I received a good amount of extra credit and he is always open to answering questions.

American Literature and Seminar: the Politics of ****graphy.  Plus 199's. He is fabulous: innovative, edgy, great sense of humor. Not an easy A but you learn and are inspired. He guides with a light touch. Some wanted more specific guidance. I didn't.

Not an affective teacher: there was no lecture or proper materials for us. He was goofy and fun and class was a blast but he didn't help with the reading material or come prepared to class. If you're used to "normal" professors, don't take him. You'll be  having a good time but HE IS A HARD GRADER; you will suffer at the end with the final essay.

analysis.name: Chris Smeenk
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
Pros: Smeenk is passionate and knowledgeable about the content and explains it well. Interesting and well-structured course content. Tested on understanding

Cons: Lack of timely communication and feedback was frustrating given how tough the course content is. Large time commitment. Unclear grading criteria at times (marking was fair though)

Great professor! I took his class at western university. It was a difficult course, but an amazing learning experience. Professor Smeenk was always available via email and even met with me to discuss material questions I had. It is clear that he really cares about his students.
Very nice, very responsive professor. However, won't recommend the course philosophy 2032 to anyone. Too much reading, so many dues, HUGE workloads, difficult exams

analysis.name: Miles Chen
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Chen is amazing! He is 1 of those professors who is very caring and wants to see his students do well, but that all depends on if you're willing to put in the time and effort to understand the material. Textbook use is low- only need for hw, this is an example of what a fair teacher, grader, and class should be like, thank you professor!

Professor miles Chen gives you the tools you need to succeed in the class ! I have not taken a math class in years but was able to pass this class. Professors Chens video lectures and tutorials on homework problems will save you in this class ! I found the text book to be confusing and useless however it is required to do homework assignments.

Stats requires at least a few hours of homework each week. You will do well without spending this time. That said, Prof Chen is a very good teacher, VERY fair grader, and offers more than enough opportunity to do well in his class (especially toward the end). Don't skip class or homework, pay close attention to lecture, and you'll excel with Chen.

In my wildest dreams I could not have imagined that I would fall in love with statistics.  Took as a prerequisite.  Its been a while since math class, so I was intimidated and thought Id have to drop out.  Dr. Chens enthusiasm, creativity, clarity and availability to answer questions motivated me to try, and I learned so much.  A great teacher!

Miles is a great teacher!! I absolutely hate math, I'm not good at it at all, but worked very very hard and got an A! He gives you a lot of hmwk, but trust me you will do very well in the class if you just keep up. He really wants the class to succeed and even posts his lectures and hmwk answers online. I recommend taking this course with him!

analysis.name: Cisca Brier
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very talented at explaining the language. Fully bilingual. Often took detours, talking about humorous personal stories and examples of Dutch culture, when she sensed the class wasn't in the mood to learn Dutch. Cares immensely about student success in the class. No textbook. You learn to converse in Dutch from Day One. Attendance IS required!

Attendance is important to her. She is an incredible person who really guides you to develop a love of learning Dutch.  This professor has a good feel for class temperament and knows when to push and when to back off. She's very compassionate. Just make sure you actually show up!

analysis.name: Ciprian Preda
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: If you haven't had Ciprian for any Math class at UCLA, you missed a lot... He leads you to understand Math concepts in fascinating way...The downward of having him as a professor is you end up comparing every other professor with him and gets disappointed. His tests are easy if you understand the concept. Final is little harder, but still manageabl
He is so nice! He really tries to make you understand math, poor guy. I understood all of the content, and whatever I didn't, he was there to help. His class was definitely not what I expected!
HE IS SO CUTE WITH HIS ACCENT AND HE IS SO NICE. I JUST LOVE HIM. GREAT TEACHER OVERALL.
The best math professor I have ever had. He teaches you to "feel" mathematics.
Most helpful teacher ever, more of a friend than a professor.

analysis.name: Claire McEachern
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is unclear in her teaching. Half the time I did not understand what she was saying because her thoughts were scattered.

Professor McEachern is a splendid professor! Her Milton lectures were like heaven to me. The first day of class she told us Milton was her favorite course to teach, and the last day of class, I cried because I could feel her love and passion for this amazing man. Milton was also a philosopher, and after this class, I felt more inclined to return to

Rating cont. as it was cut off. She is passionate about this wonderful philosophical man, and I felt my own faith stirred after taking the class. She does talk fast, so I would sit up front, come to class early and contact OSD if you have a learning disability to ask for a note taker. She is very understanding if you have an LD. See her in o.h.

She is great. Encouraging and practical. Milton is a bore but she actually makes it fun. She is easy-going and intelligent but she doesn't take herself too seriously. This is a required class for ENG majors so take it with her! But you have to do ALL the reading or you won't pass.
Claire was a phenomenal teacher.

analysis.name: Courtney Clark
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Courtney was very kind however I didn't like how she set up her midterm/final. Some questions seemed kind of vindictive as they were meant to trick you or were things like "A, B, C, A and B, A B and C" etc. I studied extremely hard for the final and thought I got 100% but ended up with a mid B. Just don't get lulled into security. NOT an easy A.

The course material was easy to understand and the professors lectures were great. However, there is a lot of work, homework twice weekly, labs, test, & journal reviews. The test questions are very confusing and answers, it seems like she goes out of her way to make the questions confusing so people can't cheat but it hurts the grades.
incredibly sweet and a great professor

analysis.name: Clara Chu
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She's totally kind, easy and informative. She doesn't really challenge the opinions of her students - no matter how silly. Conversely, she encourages everyone to chime in. Sometimes class discussion can lag while other times it can be lively. Some of her assignments - like service learning - can be lame, but she also gets you thinking.

Boring, but easy. Basically the class boils down to this: diversity is a good thing, let's unproblematically celebrate mulitculturalism. In other words: Little or no sophisticated conversation. She is extremely generous of students' comments, no matter how silly, thoughtless, or off topic.

Easy, clear instruction, and you'll actually learn something about research methods. Sometimes it can get a bit boring, especially when she reads from power point, but overall I liked the class and the professor.
As ever, Clara is great! Very helpful!

analysis.name: Claudia Parodi
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She was a really dedicated prof. who was fair with both students and faculty. We learned a lot from the area fields in which she had expertise, Spanish historical linguistics and Spanish syntax.
I'm not impressed with this instructor.  She talks too much in class but has no direction.  Grading is always a problem, and she won't listen when you make her see her own mistakes.
She's not bad teaching all the courses of the section. Grading is always a problem; expects too much and does not add all the grades correctly. Same old problem.
It is useless to take a class with her.  Her material is outdated and she is not on top of the cutting edge of Spanish linguistics.
Amazing Caring mujer, she is really knowledgeable on the field of linguistics. She is quite flexible and humanizing.

analysis.name: Claudia Rapp
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: THE FINAL IS CUMULATIVE, which is very difficult given the extensive amount of information covered.  make sure you stay on top of the readings. they are lengthy, but you WILL NEED THEM. Sections are REQUIRED and you will do well not to miss any of them because there is information given in them you cannot get anywhere else.

Lecture rooms get half empty after a few weeks.  However, she is a very intelligent teacher and is very clear.  It's not necessary to go to all the lectures though.  The midterm and the final weren't cakewalks because of the amount of material but it's easy to get an A or B because she gives you the IDs you need to study.

Dr Rapp is a wonderful professor.  She is infinitely patient and helpful.  Her insights were extremely valuable for my work - a very decent human being too.  I recommend her very strongly for graduate study.

analysis.name: Kara Cooney
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: You've got to be kidding me. Cooney was fantastic-- smart, relaxed, great sense of humor...only a midterm and a final and that's it! It's been refreshing to see a woman so young and so driven do well as an art historian and archaeologist. She totally changed my opinion on Egyptian art. She is not to be missed.

I would take her class if she was teaching the history of toilet paper. She is so fun and interesting that even though I'm not all that into Egyptian art history, I've taken two classes with her. She's hilarious and inspiring, and I totally recommend her!

Great teacher, easy to follow. She gives you all the information you need and grades liberally IF you can show that you understood the concepts in your essay's. I enjoyed her so much I took a second class with her.

Dr. Cooney has a really easy lecture style and she's very funny, which helps things stick in your head. She knows her material and is really willing to help, returns emails and will make flexible office hours if needed. Great teacher. Take any class with her.

Great professor! she's a lot of fun... very dedicated, loves her job and her students. Very helpful. Doesn't give a straight answer, "may be this, may be that... we don't know". If you love art, take her class. you won't regret.

analysis.name: Mary Corey
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She's the best!  Leans left, but this is to be expected from a professor.  She gives brilliant lectures and her office hours are some of the best times I have ever had in school.  If you do the readings, go to lectures, and to office hours then you will (probably) get an A.  I cannot say enough good things about Prof. Corey

Midterm & Finals are take home papers. She loves to teach US History of the 1960's. Not a Communist, but a Social Justice educator. Great lecture and videos (Harvey Milk & Vietnam Documentry). Also, she's very youthful & full of life experience. I always recommend this class to all my fellow Chicano student friends

She is one the GREATEST professors of history I have ever taken.  If you are having trouble, she takes the time to help you out!!  Her class is awesome!  I promise that you will not fall asleep, she captures her students with her great lectures.  I totally recommend her!

Mary was amazing.  Note to students - take notes voraciously...that helps a lot with the midterms and finals...regurgitation of lecture + your well formulated and intellectual opinion of the subject matter = A...don't be brain dead and you will be fine.  Also, take advantage of office hours...this is where you will get some of the most intellecutally stimulating conversation she has to offer.  If you want the chance to speak with a very accomplished and interesting Prof, this is her.

If you get the chance to take a class with Mary, jump at it. She is one of the best professors I have ever had, a true delight to learn from, passionate and quick-witted. She thrives on teaching and it shows. She does assign a lot of reading, but as long as you're engaged you should be fine. Also: GO TO OFFICE HOURS! You'll miss out if you don't.

analysis.name: William Joseph Conley
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Conley is one of the best professors I have ever had. He cares a lot about his students and will voluntarily take time out of his schedule to make sure that you understand everything. While his tests are challenging, they really test students' knowledge and understanding. Take Conley if you're looking to really learn!

He is extremely helpful in helping with homework and studying for tests. Very nice teacher. High recomend him. His quizzes are fair, but tests can become difficult, especially in the final which caught many people off guard. He explains things very well and makes attending class worth it.
he's a very nice teacher, pretty easy lectures and straightforward. He's very helpful! definitely recommend him! HW is not as bad either and quizzes are pretty fair.

Conley is great and really cares. You should study before quizzes and tests. As long as you put in a little time you will experience the rewards. Amazing professor and really explains everything so that everyone cane have the opportunity to understand what he is teaching.
Best professor I had in my freshman year at UCLA, engaging lectures and extremely helpful review sessions. Can't recommend him enough.

analysis.name: Michael Colacurcio
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really loved his lectures but you must do the reading in order to follow along otherwise good luck! He gives quizzes but 2 are take home. He gives you study guides for the final and a sufficient amount of time for essays. Easy grader as long as you put in some effort. 20% of the grade is just showing up to class! I think I'll get an A!

He is cynical and shocking but he is 100% involved in the learning of students. There are handouts, a reader and practice quizzes. He tells you what he wants for the tests, does close analysis on the texts and engages the class. VERY GOOD PERSON, very intelligent and very efficient. Straight forward class!

Prof. Cola is a very intellingent man.  He does not tip his hand when teaching.  He expects you to learn, do the reading and participate.  I really enjoyed having an intimate seminar with one of the world's leading Hawthorne experts.  He is eager to see his students do well.

Professor Colacurcio was really nice and funny. He gives easy quizzes--he basically hand a quiz to you you a few weeks in advance and you just have to listen for the answers. He is an easy grader too. I got an A in his class.
Professor Colacurcio is a man of staggering genius. If you really want to learn anything in an English course and you are not afraid to work, sign up for one of his courses.

analysis.name: Ferdinand Coroniti
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Didn't necessarily feel like the lecture was all that valuable to the class. I did fine by spending that time reading the textbook rather than attending. He tells you the concepts for the exams in advance, though. Midterms are fairly simple- ranging in easy to somewhat hard questions. There's no homework or quizzes, just two midterms and final.
Professor Coroniti is an awesome guy that is genuinely interested in and knowledgeable in his field. There were no homework assignments in the Astronomy 5 class, and it was very interesting.

analysis.name: Craig Schroeder
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: why did you take my rating down Mr craig what's up with that
Sigma Sigma on the wall, who's the rizziest of them all.

Quiz every single week. Doesn't matter if it's midterm week or the week before finals. You're still going to have a quiz. But don't expect to get these quizzes back because that would be ridiculous. Started doing better in the class after I stopped going. 10 week quarter, haven't been to class in 6 weeks.

analysis.name: Milo Crimi
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Milo is very caring and is willing to work with you if you are struggling. The class is difficult if you struggle with math, but Milo will help you every step of the way. All the exams are take-home, and all the homework is collaborative. There are plenty of extra credit opportunities. Great class!

Milo is incredibly kind and an amazing, engaging teacher. He is super knowledgeable about the material he teaches and is willing to thoughtfully answer all your questions. The grading is very much based on the effort you put into his class. The homework is relatively light, but it's important that you do it. One of my favorite professors so far! :)

Professor Crimi is awesome. You can tell he really cares for his students and wants everyone to succeed. He is flexible and very accessible outside the classroom. I loved taking Formal Logic with him and would recommend taking his class!

analysis.name: Michelle Craske
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I am now in grad school in another state but I still use the text and remember what a great prof she was.  She is not boring, just low-key.  Approach for help and she will. Loved the class. Taking a grad version of it this coming Fall.
Extremely knowledgable about anxiety disorders-if you show willingness to learn and grow-she will support you all the way!
Dr. Craske may seem hard to approach or too quiet and shy.  But approach her any way and you'll find that she's amazing!
Dr. Craske my seen hard to approach, but when you do, you'll find that she is amazing.
Not really supportive. Got nowhere with her after a long time.

analysis.name: Jackson Crawford
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He really cares about what he taught and made sure everyone understood it.
Wow, amazing course, amazing professor. The most fun you'll probably have in a UCLA class. Handsome too, that never hurts.

Professor Crawford's classes were a highlight of my time at UCLA. Probably his best quality as a teacher is his desire to make Old Norse literature and mythology accessible and interesting to laypeople. At least with me, he definitely succeeded.
He's a great professor and really wants students to care about the subjects as much as he does. I've taken a few classes with him because it's either fun or interesting or both!
He knew he was working with difficult material and tried his best to make it clear and concise.

analysis.name: Dana Cuff

Critical thinking is not encouraged; students must regurgitate her views. She makes illogical connections, creating an uncomfortable atmosphere for open discussion.

This professor demonstrates a lack of intelligence, evident in her assignments of useless content and attempts to indoctrinate students with her personal agenda. Additionally, she shows clear preferential treatment towards certain students, which undermines fairness in the classroom.

This professor demonstrates a lack of care, and constantly tries to attempts to indoctrinate students with her personal agenda in a way that is so obvious, everyone is left very uncomfotable. Additionally, she shows clear preferential treatment towards some students over others.
All this professor's classes are useless and it's clear that she is only in the Department because otherwise she would be unemployed.
Absoloutely horrible. A complete waste of time.

analysis.name: Sam Cumming
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit

review_highlights.comment: Sam was great and I highly recommend him to future students. There were three 4-page papers, and participation - each accounted for 25% of the grade. He is a very forgiving grader and he gives very helpful feedback on papers. Class discussions were great. I really appreciated how he treated students. He genuinely cared about our opinions/ideas

He is such a sweet teacher and exceptionally intelligent. He just recently published a great piece of philosophical work in 2011 and really shows true potential as a professor and mentor. Plus, he's super attractive and from Australia! Too bad he's married!

he's great at explaining things in office hours, but his ramblings during class are really hard to understand....pretty much the only thing that keeps you attentive is the fact that he's really hot and has a sexy accent!
He's such a sweet guy! Very interesting lectures, just really hard to follow--you need need need to ask a lot of questions. He's really smart but kind of bad at explaining things orally....
Rutgers guy and very smart. He is young and a bit unseasoned but overall a promising teacher and researcher. I left being very interested in philosophy of language and communication.

analysis.name: Samuel Culbert
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can

Because he so hates getting feedback of any kind (his articles in WSJ are not subject to reader comments) he has tried to vilify any docrine which advocates for feedback as a means to improve performance. Teach, don't preach!
He is the ultimate jerk. He is in love with himself. You learn lot of dogmas- not functional at all
Smug; indifferent toward his students; and a poor teacher, that is overly impressed with himself.

analysis.name: Cynthia Lebow

She will rarely if ever, delve into the required course readings that are both excessive and frequently irrelevant to her actual assignments. Her student responses range from passive-aggressive to outright rude. Do not take her class.

One of those classes that are hard to get through but once you have you walk away appreciating it. The vast amount of knowledge you will gain about the Court system, major cases, the federal government, and law terminology makes this class worthwhile. Especially if your planning on law school. Lebow is attentive,clear and ready to help in any way

Professor Lebow can be best described as fair. I have taken 3 of her classes and in all three of her classes, there has been a lot of reading and a lot of content. If you are willing to put in the effort, you will get an A. she is not out to trick you. Professor Lebow is also always willing to help her students.

On the first day of class Lebow said her class might not be for you if you have a "boyfriend or a job". I literally have to work to survive. The work load from this class is unmanageable, you can only keep your sanity if this is your only class for the quarter. Avoid at all costs.
2 stories: 1. A student asks for advice and is told to just drop the course. 2. A student asks for clarification and is accused of not attending class. Enough Said.

He's a nice enough guy and he wants to help but his French accent is extremely heavy and if you're not already good at calculus, his lectures and even office hours can be of limited help because of his limited English.
Decent prof, but I don't think the board is what the girls in his class stare at during his lectures.
What can I say? Smart + attractive guy= great prof!

analysis.name: David Stahlman

So basically there was litewrally this one time where hew as like teaching and then he gave a test. SO literally gotta study like literally

Bad teacher, but not terrible. He gives the impression that his class is hard to pass, but he want simple and easy to read essays. WEIRD coming from a UCLA professor. He will grade you down if you use big words. Overall, try to avoid his courses.

If you have anxiety, this class is perfect for you. You never get called on unless you volunteer. With that being said, if you are not an auditory learner, it'll be a pretty hard class for you. He goes off book or notes he writes and it's hardly on the board (just record the lecture on goodnotes and go back as many times as you want).
Fantastic professor. Knowledgable about literature, art, philosophy, history, and even popular culture. Loved this class.

The subject material is fascinating. It revolves around author Henry James, his life and major works. For this class, you'll have to read five or six of his books and the way he writes can be very confusing. The professor is not extremely helpful in this arena, but his lectures are interesting.

analysis.name: Jared Diamond
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Yes, as an avid reader of Diamond, and a faculty member at another institution, I too am surprised to see no ratings on Diamond. He is like a real Indiana Jones. We need more like him in higher ed.

Diamond is, well, a gem. He imparts soooo much incredible information, and you remember years (and I would assume decades) later. I had him for two classes, both of which you must write an essay just to get into, well worth the effort. I feel fortunate to have been in his classes, he won't be around forever! Pay attention in class and you do well!

I can't believe no one has rated jared Diamond. He is one of the most famous professors in the country and a legend at UCLA. When you enter a mind like that you don't come out the same person. You will have a very different view on humanity and you can die knowing that you were taught by one of the greatest mind of century.

Very fun and engaging lectures, as well as the Discussion Sections. The homework is easy and straightforward, but not overwhelming and also relevant. The class does start out a bit slow, but the articles you read and write about are all interesting and fun to discuss in class and in Discussion.
He's amazing. So nice & insightful

He is a very good professor and makes the material understandable. Not a very easy class, but you will learn a lot, and with effort, you will earn a high grade.
Modern Political Theory - tough class.  The Professor and TA's however, are great. Highly recommend this Professor.
he's amazing. honestly he changed my life

My experience with this teacher rates lowest of all my experiences with teachers at UCLA. He leads the anthropology departmental honors, and in my opinion, is better suited to teaching kindergarten. In a program designed to give the most interested and advanced students in the department an advantage, he insisted on mindless handholding and busywor

I was an Anthro major many moons ago at UCLA and Dr. Hollan was one of my favorite. I found his course challenging, entertaining, and he was a very approachable Professor. I never met a student who did not like him! He's a wonderful human being. Period.

analysis.name: Sharon Dolovich

review_highlights.comment: Professor Dolovich is brilliant!  Had heard mixed things about her entering the class, but she really surprised me with how interesting the class ended up being.  What really held the discussions back, at least in my section, was actually the class' unwillingness to oppose "PC" opinions, for fear of offending someone.

Amazing. Absolutely brilliant, and even though she talks REALLY fast (!), she is very willing to work with you outside of class if you don't understand the issues. She focused on very interesting topics within Crim. I came into the class not interested in the subject and now I'm absolutely in love! Dolovich was the best prof I had this semester.
Her exam is pretty easy - 8 hour take home.  A lot of smart people did poorly in her class. =)

This woman had managed to completely turn off any interest I might have had in criminal law. She was more interested in dragging out of us what she thinks the policy should be than cases and law. Too bad as 1Ls people don't have much choice to avoid her.

Do attend class and try to stay somewhat engaged; she tends to call on people that appear to be zoned out. Write well and edit your exam; there is no time pressure and the issues are easy to spot, so clear writing sets you apart.

analysis.name: Stephen Dickey
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: If you can write well, I fully recommend taking ENGL 91A (Intro to Poetry) with Dr. D. Preferably, take it your first quarter at UCLA, because you can really get a chance to meet people and interact with the class. Sometimes lectures are slow, but this class is 100% what you make of it. Read poems, write a paper, and take two exams (test bank!).

AMAZING instructor. His sense of humor and personality shine through in his lectures. Definitely my most highly enjoyed class, ever. He makes learning fun and therefore easy to remember. He is so passionate and knowledgeable--  I could not imagine being taught Shakespeare by anyone else...see for yourself what everyone is raving about!!

Professor Dickey has been by far one the most amazing professor I have had. He is very inspiring, knowledgable, and passionate on what he teaches. His engagement with his students is remarkable and he is always happy to assist student. I cannot emphasizes enough how much I appreciate to have had the opportunity to be taught Shakespeare by this guy!

People with anxiety listen up: as someone with severe anxiety this class wasn't bad. He doesn't expect people to share out or call on people at random. He does do popcorn reading through each row sometimes but the lines are never long and no one looks up from their papers. he's really nice and it wasn't a bad experience. *Join the groupme it helps*

Professor Dickey is my favorite Professor at UCLA.  I have never met anyone who is genuinely interested and knowledgeable in so many subjects.  If you put in the effort and get to know him, you will not only leave the class with lots of knowledge, but a helpful and kind friend.

analysis.name: Donald Neuen

review_highlights.comment: Many graduate level music classes for conducting majors at UCLA.  Be prepared!  Do your best!  Soak up all he has to offer. Even if it seems extreme (perhaps?), you will be able to apply it in the future. No nonsense. Full of artistry, talent, and methods that WORK!

An inspirational professor who is a breath of fresh air in this world where society beats around the bush, gets nothing accomplished, and then congratulates themselves.  Neuen is the real deal with the experience and international accolades to prove it.

brilliant... one of the most interesting and insightful professors i've ever encountered... always has useful and pertinent commentary on almost every topic a student could come up with... an extremely talented conductor and evangelical teacher...  one of shaw's most brilliant protoges.
Worth living in LA because of him.

review_highlights.comment: In a field of study that has a tendency to be confusing more often that not, it helps to have a professor that is willing to explain things until everyone has an understanding. Professor Duhart knows how to communicate ideas,command a classroom, and be approachable at the same time. He encourages questions and creates a noble learning environment.

One of the best professors at CSULA. He is too smart for his own good. Extremely passionate about his lectures and gets students engaged. Talks fast as hell so try and keep up. The readings are complicated and semi-boring but once you hear him explain it, it all makes so much sense. No text books, everything online.

Duhart is one of those professors that reminds you why you're majoring in sociology. He is engaging during lectures, and clears up any difficult concepts. He assigned one midterm, final, and weekly memos from the previous weeks  readings. Material he utilizes is very educating.

He is really knowledgeable. He lectures with passion and enthusiasm. There is a couple of assignments but he does give clear instructions on what he wants and what he looking for. Will fail students for not citing correctly or at all.

I regularly tell people about things I learned in 187A/187B, and it's years later; that's how interesting it was.  Whether you're psych, pre-law, or poli-sci -- take it!!

The good: knows his stuff, offers help The bad: class kinda boring, can't hear his voice

take Kung if you want to boost your gpa. I took sproul for 1 and 11 and loved him and did well, but Kung is even easier than sproul. Two midterms and a final. He's a little quiet when he lectures but always willing to help during office hours

Everything she says is worded so beautifully, she'll be lecturing, weaving her tale of truth and justice, you're just sitting in awe like 'YAS TELL 'EM SIS'.

Professor Eileraas has been, HANDS DOWN, the greatest professor at USC I have had so far. Everything that comes out of her mouth is intelligent, sharp, and clearcut. Her lectures are long and she does assign a lot of dry readings (the ones I read were v interesting.) Have to be interested in the subject or she will bore you. PARTICIPATION MATTERS!

Dr. Eileraas is a wonderful professor with an amazing ability to articulate her lectures clearly and challenge student's generalized thinking. Her lectures satisfy many different learning techniques as she incorporates music, quotes, videos, additional literature and great insight concerning the subject material. The absolute best professor ever!

An extremely helpful professor. Cares about learning. Seems somewhat unapproachable but very pleasant if you do. Teaches all sides of feminist theory without personal bias. One of the best WS professors at UCLA.

very interesting and different kind of class.

Thank you, Dr. Dauer for a great class! This class is one of those few classes where what you learn you use for life to help yourself and/or others. Dr. Dauer teaches extremely well, so hard concepts are made clear. He also takes time to speak with students and help them on their career pathway.

Dr. Dauer has so much life experience and I felt very lucky to have been able to learn something from him over this course! His lectures can be pretty heavy and include some techniques/procedures with immense detail that he breezes over though. However, I appreciated him letting us know exactly what we needed to study and his straightforwardedness.

Great Professor, makes course material really interesting. Very qualified, over qualified if anything to teach. An honor to learn from a man who has so many degrees. Caring, willing to offer help and advice. Really interesting to learn course material from the various perspectives he has to offer. Sympathetic towards student learning and obstacles.

I took Dr. Dauer  for Physiologic Sciences 122 in summer session A at UCLA during summer 2017. I really cant say enough good things about Dr. Dauer.  He is kind, knowledgable, funny, and very fair as a professor.  You will learn tons of physiology as well as essential lessons when applying to medical school.  You are the best Dr. Dauer. Thank you!

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Keeler is an excellent professor. He uses a variety of methods to ensure that you learn the material. He is particularly good at teaching math and economics concepts. He uses a lot of real-world examples. One of the things I appreciated the most is how he asked us repeatedly to give him feedback on the course so that he could improve it.

Professor Keeler is very approachable and asks for feedback after every class. He responds promptly to student questions and suggestions and try to be helpful by providing more explanations. Sometimes the economic/math concepts are difficult to come across to health science students. Many terminologies take some time to get used to.

Dr. Keeler was a great professor. His experiences at RAND and real-life examples made this class very applicable and useful training for the real world! Highly recommend taking his course. And, he is super nice.
I have far too many years of education, and Dr. Keeler is one of the best professors I've ever had.

Noel Enyedy is my favorite uncle

analysis.name: Erin Hartman

review_highlights.comment: Expectations are waaay too high. She kind of rushes you, which is not conducive to learning such hard stuff in a thorough way. Professor kind of expects you to be a coding whiz and about 90% of the material will go over your head. Most of class is spent asking for help and the readings are never clear & go over your head, which makes quizes hard.

Don't take this class with her. Worst professor I have had. Not a good instructor; visibly annoyed when you ask questions or help solving a problem. Lectures from slides and textbook, does not walk you through anything. Group work, worksheets, academic papers. You know it's bad when the TAs don't know how to answer the questions on the worksheets.

Unless you have taken statistics before, or just enjoy teaching yourself material; don't take this class. Hartman is smug, and makes a clear effort to be condescending especially to those who are clearly struggling with the material. She's a smart person, but she absolutely should not be teaching an undergraduate lower division class.
Stay away from this class. She does not care about her students and is truly the WORST teacher I have ever taken. She does not know how to teach and is too lazy to explain anything to her students.

I did not enjoy taking this class. In fact, if you're a poli sci major (or any major besides computer science) I would highly recommend taking just a regular stats class to fill your requirements. Professor Hartman places so much emphasis on learning coding that the part where you actually learn stats gets neglected and must be self taught.

analysis.name: Eric Jager
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty

review_highlights.comment: Jäger is a very passionate professor. Jager's lectures are always entertaining and his energy makes you want to participate even if you're usually the "quiet" one. Jager is very clear on the grading criteria and as long as you do the reading, you'll be fine. Definitely recommend Jager for Medieval lit but I heard he is great for Chaucer too.

Jager displays a deep interest in his course material and can really suck in those who have had limited experience previously with Anglo-Saxon literature.  His course covering Beowulf is extremely interesting.  His requirements for letters of rec are bogus though, as he tends to make up ridiculous reasons not to write them for straight-A students

Prof Jager is a charismatic, inspiring teacher who brings Chaucer alive to his students. He really cares about student learning. He even gave an invaluable workshop to help improve our writing skills. If you keep up-to-date with your reading each week then your Middle English will really improve, plus you will be well prepared for the final.

AMAZING PROFESSOR! He makes medieval literature very interesting and also quite modern for us millennials. He's also very accommodating and helpful whenever you approach him for any help or feedback. I definitely recommend this professor because he will challenge you while enriching your literature learning experience.

Unlike some other professors I've taken, he seems to realize that he's dealing with full-time students and so doesn't assign a ridiculous amount of reading. Everything is manageable. You DO have to keep up with the reading, though, but overall he's a great professor who's really fair and considerate.

analysis.name: Joseph Esdin
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
