review_highlights.comment: He must be writing his own reviews on this site. I took an online Endocrinology class with him. Basically, he assigned you a bunch of chapters from an Endocrinology textbook and gives you multiple choice tests that could cover anything from the textbook. Gave very limited handouts and minimal guidance. Instead of paying tuition, just buy the book.

I have taken 3 classes by Dr. Esdin, and I look forward to taking all his other classes! I took Endocrin, Pulmonary Physio, and Cardiovascular Pathophys. Very caring professor. He takes the extra time to make it seem more like an in-person class rather than online class. This rare esp in other UC online classes that I have taken many of.

He is very punctual with E-Mail replies. Also, he is very clear about his expectations and of the material that students need to know for class. Do not wait until the last min to study. Study as planned in syllabus, you will do great. Buy the book, worth it. He gives review sheets, very helpful. Weekly quizzes, easy. Exams:use review sheet as guide

Dr. Esdin is superlative. His lectures are perfect. He is very aware of himself, the students, and guides the class masterfully and seamlessly. His class is truly a masterpiece of teaching. He is clearly passionate about the genetics as well as teaching, a great combination. One of the best professors I've ever had the pleasure of learning under.

For Physci 5 you have to sign in for participation during discussions. My lectures were 50 minutes three times a week and section is once a week for 2 hours(lab).  The class was manageable and Professor Esdin was a great lecturer. He sometimes makes silly comments.  It's easy to keep up if you make sure to read along and go to lectures.

analysis.name: Randy Fallows
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Randy is very different than other English college professors. The fact that we can call him "Randy" instead of Mr. Fallows is awesome. You get graded on participation and it's great because it's not intimidating to participate when the class is a small group. The essays he makes us write were related to things happening in our world too.

Contrary to what the last person wrote, Randy does not have a large ego.  He asks to be called by his first name, elicits students for their opinions (rather than pushes his), and admits that he doesn't know all the answers,  His main goal is to get you to think for yourself, rather than repeat his opinions like most professors ask you to do.

not your average class. I took Prof F. for an SMC extension class. I may only be a freshman, but so far he's much different than the other professors. Never condescending and genuinely tries to help you out. Just read the plays, write commentaries, write a play/essay and you'll do fine. Easy and Interesting =best class so far

Awesome professor and extremely helpful. But don't expect to turn in a paper and get an A right the way. He will make you revise it a couple of times. But you have control over your grades since he lets you revise your papers til you get the grade that you want.
Professor Fallows is amazing!His classes are fun and he is very mellow. He lets you make up work if you miss a class, and his main focus is to get you to think about the text you read! take him!

analysis.name: Juliet Falce-Robinson
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Best Spanish Professor at UCLA! Everything you cover in the class is on the test, so there are no surprises which makes the class easy if you just pay attention and do the work! I have no complains about her teaching method.

The structure of her class is simple and interesting. She is a family person that always has a new picture of her kids as her computer background. Although she is busy by being in charge of the TA's for every lower division course in the department she is surprisingly very accessible either for office hours in her office or for virtual office hours

Amazing teacher! would recommend her to anyone. We should have more teachers like her. She is so helpful, makes you work to ensure you are learning. Genuinely cares about her students and takes time to get to know each one. Makes sure you are on track and more than willing to help. Very smart, funny, charismatic and all around wonderful!

analysis.name: Peter Felker
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Just a terrible lecturer. Puts you to sleep the second you walk into the lecture hall and what he says has nothing to do with the homework or the midterms and finals (which were next to impossible). They often say that open note tests are harder than usual... well try open textbook with felker. That just gives you a feel for how tough his class is

Lectures were convoluted and did little if anything to clarify material, relying almost exclusively on derivations. Rarely took questions and was very unapproachable, seemingly annoyed if anyone had questions. His tests were entirely mathematical, a direct contrast from his derivational lectures. A mess of a lecturer.
His lecture is very bad. His tests are extremly hard....If you don't want to ruin your gpa or suffer from his classes, please stay away from him!!
Lectures are boring. It's pretty much the same as him reading the book. Only way to pass is to go to sections and try to teach yourself.
If you aren't stupid and go meet with him during his office hours he is very helpful and you will do well

analysis.name: Felicity Nussbaum
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She's professional, she knows her stuff, and she even has a sense of humor to go along with it.  Her tests weren't overly difficult.  Good lecturer.
She is not only brilliant, but she is able to teach and help students understand the brilliant concepts she conveys.
She is not only brilliant, but she is able to communicate her briliance to students in a way they can understand.
She is an amazing Professor! Not only was she interesting, but she was clear and brilliant!

analysis.name: Eric Scerri
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 42 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Awful teacher. Incredibly disorganized: makes mistakes 10+ times a lecture despite the fact that he's been teaching this subject for years. His course reader was like Egyptian hieroglyphics I had to decipher, and sadly his tests are entirely based on the ridiculously confusing course reader. Does not seem to care about his students, just his ego.

Everybody complains about Dr. Scerri's snobby demeanor, but honestly, I found it hilarious. Apart from that, he's a really good professor. He WILL lose you at times, as his course reader becomes hard to follow in some places. But if you pay attention, seek help when it's needed and do the practice tests in the back of the reader, you WILL pass.
Ok, not the best or the worst. I took his class because the other professor was even worse.
Definitely recommend over other 14B teachers: not too much hw, lectures relatively easy to understand, and you can prepare for the midterm and final by looking through past ones.

I feel like I'm always interrupting him when I visit Prof Scerri during his office hours.  It's like he quickly wants to get rid of you and go back to whatever is on his computer.  In class, you'll notice that the more attractive a girl is the more attention she gets.  He seems very cool and aloof in his European sort of way.  Can't figure him out.

analysis.name: Rahul Fernandez
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

analysis.name: Robert Fink
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I was not a music major, but was able to take his amazing class on current music and its roots. Totally amazing. I would listen to him read the phone book! I learned a ton. Probably the best class I took at UCLA.
though only music students will probably ever have him, he is a great professor.  His was one of my favorite classes at UCLA
Very articulate. His lectures are easy to understand and help very well on the exams.

analysis.name: Keith Fink
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: One of the top professors at school. Dr. Fink gets beyond the doublethink and newspeak prevalent on campus and analyzes first amendment rights in a clear, compelling, and interesting way.  Sooner or later, administration will likely figure out a way to get rid of him, because he's not politically correct. Take his course now.

Took him in the summer and he was fantastic . Don't leave UCLA without taking a course with him. He truly cares and cases our really interesting. He goes over the cases in class. The class wasn't difficult and very enjoyable.

Best class I have had. Professor extremely dymamic and smart and teaches in a unique pure Socratic style. Professor Fink went to UCLA and clearly loves the school and teaching. Very interesting material covered and I came away with a great understanding of the First Amendment.

Great guy. Teaches in a very direct and easy to understand method. Loves to debate interesting hypotheticals. Fair warning though, he is politically incorrect, so the school will probably try to get rid of him soon, so sign up for his class NOW

Keith Fink is one of the best Professors I've ever had at UCLA. It is troubling to me that at this time Fink is being forced out of UCLA. I don't like what I'm hearing or reading on the subject and this prevalent fabrication of evidence. He has a mastery of the material, makes lectures interesting and interactive&makes himself readily available

analysis.name: Max Flomen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: We were graded on two papers. For the first assignment, we were required to use our lecture notes and the four to five sources he provided. Since Max's lectures are filled with information and his slides are 95% photos...I would recommend recording him, if possible. I enjoyed the subject and material, but I hope he changes the course in the future.

Prof. Flomen is extremely knowledgeable, and it shows in his lectures. He is passionate about what he teaches. Lots of reading, many papers, and participation is important. I learned so much from him.
Great lectures, interesting readings, and extremely knowledgeable. His class was one of the best UCLA courses I've taken.

analysis.name: Susan Foster
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the most arrogant and insecure human beings you will ever meet.  Veneer of manufactured personability covering for a lack of actual concern for students and impossibly self-centered behavior.

The other raters are right. Stay away from this woman's class. The grading is based on sucking up. She doesn't seem to prepare lectures, just randomly talk on the topic, beginning discussions of tough theoretical topics not at the beginning by explaining baseline info, but halfway through. Also, very rude to students who disagree with her opinions

Susan Foster is the best teacher in the department. She genuinely cares about her students. Is the class easy? No. But like any other class, it requires work. She helped me become a better dancer, writer, and artist-scholar and I know many that feel as I do. I highly recommend her class to all!
If you want a good grade, better agree with her, no dissenting opinions tolerated.

analysis.name: Wendy Fonarow
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: There were certain things that were a plus about her as a professor and certain things that were a minus - HATED that she viewed our assignments as a contract and was rigid in this regard but she was very engaging and equally as fair when it came to grading.

Fonarow is a kickass Professor. Her lectures are so engaging- I always looked forward to her class. She's funny too and very eloquent. She cares about her students and even likes hanging out with them. A year after her class- I'm still on her emaillist.

This was a terrible class. I love anthropology, but I hated this class. her lectures are horrible. She drones and is so boring. try to take another class instead of this. if this is an elective, do NOT take it.

analysis.name: Caroline Ford
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Ford is exceedingly knowledgeable, and it was an honor to learn from her in a small 15-student seminar. (She's also very kind!) If you take Paris: Biography of a City, be prepared for 200-400 pages of reading a week, an 8-page midterm paper, and a 12-15 page research paper. It is A LOT of work, but it's worth it.

While the class is easy, the lectures are unbearable. Professor Ford is very descriptive, but often includes unimportant details. She is also extremely boring. While reading the textbook the material was rather interesting but when I got to lecture it was a struggle to not fall asleep.

Best  class ever. 10 percent map quiz . 2o percent participation.  20 percent short paper. 30 percent final and 20 percent midterm. Very straight forword class.Alex is a highly recommended ta. Easy A if u try. professor is a sweetheart!

It seemed evident to me the Ford had interesting analysis on the topics, but they were presented adequately. Lectures are monotone and can be hard to sit through without getting distracted. In my section I was assigned a 500 word response to readings every week, there were two map quizzes, and two essays.
Cannot recommend this Professor.  Highly inconsistent grading standards - petty and conveniently subjective.

analysis.name: Stephen Frank
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very well prepared lectures.  The material was an excellent selection of primary sources -absolutely facinating information.  I LOVED this class and Professor Frank - looked forward to classes every week.

Really good professor. He is not intimidating to talk to and is very willing to listen to his students during office hours or after class. YOU. MUST. DO. THE. READINGS. The class is a breeze if you do. Easy grader as long as you don't summarize the content and have some idea what you're talking about. Lectures are very helpful, just pay attention.

This guy seriously doesn't know how to teach. Might as well go party and @#$% Russian chicks. He nearly never reply to our emails and always acting so arrogant when teaching. His lectures r BORING AS HELL!! Many people even slept in class. He NEVER engage the class to the materials were learning! So useless to even take Russian History with him!

Constantly and desperately seeks validation through contrived jokes, will openly disparage a class non-complicit with this vanity. His powerpoints seem custom-made to detract from lecture cohesion and confuse students. Not a bad person - Just a bad professor.
He's a friendly professor who gives solid lectures.

analysis.name: Rachel Fretz
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: It's a challenging course for those of you that, like me, struggle with writing. She assigns long papers and is a tough grader. However, if you ask for help, she's always nice and willing to help. If you're good with writing and reading you'll be fine; I could've gotten an A. I wouldn't take it again but it was definitely a nice experience.

Dr. Fretz comes off to me like a genuinely nice lady. There's a LOT of work and she's VERY tough when it comes to grading, but she gives thorough feedback on how to improve. There's also the nice thing of being able to drop your first essay if it tanks your grade (You write 3 in the quarter and the first one's 30%)
TOO much work. Her projects are interesting, but assignments are due every other day! Let me breathe, lady!
Way too much work bruhhh. Its not worth it.

analysis.name: Anthony Friscia
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took him for Physiology and learned so much. Keep your head down, listen, read, study. His lectures are great and overwhelming, just know that is your 2nd time hearing the material (read chapter before class) and once you review the lecture again it will all set in.  He can seem a bit standoffish but that is just him being awkward science guy.

Very unorganized. Doesn't stay on track in lectures so hard to follow. No homework, no attendance. About 6 under 5 question quizzes. Not worth much in the grade though. Grade based on a midterm and cumulative final for the most part.Tests are mostly essay.
His lecture are not hard to understand and have key information on the core of the class and tests. However, there are just TOO MANY THINGS to memorize, and he does not care.

analysis.name: Saul Friedlander
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took his Holocaust class (183B), got an A- and loved it. I thought it'd be great to take his history seminar, but it wasn't nearly as good. 5 of the 15 students ended up being grad students who got a lot of the focus and changed the whole dynamic. He assigned a lot of reading and then never really had us do anything with it. 183B was GREAT!

I am a Psychology major, interested in the Holocaust & decided to take his class. He has changed my aversivess to learning History. He tells History like a story-- he is factual & encompasses all antecedents to understand outcomes. I wish I could learn more from him. Fascinating class and an honor to be taught by this man.

Friedlander is brilliant. If you are going to learn Holocaust history from anyone it should be him, there are few that are better qualified. I don't really inderstand the full of himself comment, I suppose if someone knows what they are talking about then they must be full of themselves.

i took 183A and 183B. the Man is brilliant and extremely insightful. 183A is easier than 183B. Great courses. Attendance and taking good notes is mandatory. Get to know ur reader since they read ur exams, NOT Friedlander.
A world renowned historian on the holocaust. His background is also very interesting: Read When Memory Comes. Helpful man, really.

analysis.name: Frederick Park
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great class: the subject was fun (in a word:3d), and the teacher explained it phenomenally(blatant sycophancy). He doesn't curve his tests because he doesn't have to: you'll most definitely get at least 90s on his tests because he has practice exams, review sessions and good lectures. Smith is the best TA around so if you fail it's your damn fault.

Park really cares for his class to learn and do well on the tests. He thoroughly goes over the material and his lecture notes facilitate note-taking. His tests are fair, but not easy. Everyone does well though.

analysis.name: Jonathan Furner
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Furner is one of those rare professors who both cares about his research and very much wants you to care (or at least think long and hard) about it too. I thought he did an excellent job of facilitating class discussing and curbing it when it was getting too heated or going astray.
Really good.  Covers a lot of material, but keeps it understandable and interesting.
Friendly, clear, interesting, funny. Really enjoyed his class.

analysis.name: Gary Gray
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: What a joke. Transfer to USC and study with a real clarinet teacher, please.
He is a very easy going guy, although this does not deter him from giving constructive advice. Note: You need to be self-critical because he usually won't do that for you.

analysis.name: Rachel George
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Grade based on: Participation (which included turning in a question at the end of class and doing weekly read and response assignments), midterm, and a final paper. She is an engaging lecturer and very clear in her expectations.

She is a grad student, but still totally inept to be teaching a college level course. Her lectures were all over the place, the exam questions were on things that were nowhere in the course material, and she was extremely pretentious and unapproachable. I managed to get an A in the course, but really wish I'd taken it with a better professor.

Grade consisted of two things: participation (doing weekly reading assignments, attending class, engaging in class, a powerpoint presentation) and a final paper. The final paper carried most of the wight and was centered around data analysis. This is a writing intensive class. She is a good professor who is clear in her expectations.

This whole class is one in-the-field research project ending in a 15 page paper. Very fun, lots of independent work, and amazing discussions.

10/10 Professor. Not too hard but a lot of work.

analysis.name: George Baker
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: His lectures are full of energy but like most large classes he uses a TA which are NOT as intelligent and no where near as exciting to listen to. He is a little on the pompous side. I enjoyed the class but won't choose him again as an instructor.

He is so smart and into art history, which makes his lectures really interesting. But some of the concepts he talks about are hard to grasp quickly and he talks fast, making note taking sort of hard. And there was SOOOOO much reading for this class. He seemed nice but intimidatingly smart and somewhat condescending. The grading made it hard to tell

Professor Baker is really one of the most amazing teachers at UCLA. I've learned so much from his lectures and he has really inspired me to think more deeply about art. He does however have a terrible habit of Duchamping people. The Duchamping is a bit much, even if he follows up with the appropriated laughter meme. Fair grader, Good commenter.

Lectures and scholarly articles are everything. As usual w AH courses, profs give more articles per week than is humanly possible to read. But why I'm really here is to talk about his lectures: listening to him talk is like trying to decode a cipher. His convoluted verbiage overcomplicates topics and comes off as pompous. Skip this prof if you can.

Without a doubt the most thrilling lecturer I have ever experienced.  He is a rare, rare breed.  A little heavy on the phallic references, but hey, this only makes him more attractive.  Beautiful shoes, beautiful mind, but ugly course load, oh my god did we READ.  Very challenging studying for his tests: you WON'T be able to predict test questions.

analysis.name: Gerardo Lavina
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Gerry is popular with a lot of students - but mainly with the ones he's cool with. He has his favorites in the department, and often funnels them into the better internships/fellowships. He has also shown himself to have pretty questionable boundaries with students, often drinking with them and flirting with female students. Don't believe the hype.

The most unprofessional encounter in my academic career was with Professor Laviña. I came to his office prior to beginning my MSW for help with an issue concerning my placement. Mr Laviña made accommodations but also, without good reason, made it sound as though my ability to continue with my MSW more broadly was at risk. I wish I reported him then

Gerry is just awesome! He cares a lot about his students. We love him so much his office hours are always booked because of students wanting to get some time with him. I've cried a few times during this quarter and Gerry was always there to encourage me to keep going. He is also a pretty awesome lecturer. Better than the rest if you ask me.

Gerry is an exceptional professor, mentor and social worker. He is an amazing asset to UCLA, the Social Welfare department and this profession. He is a genuinely caring individual that goes above and beyond to support his students.

analysis.name: Tom Gillespie
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: A lot of memorization and a lab poster project that was too artsy for my taste.  Lectures were great, though-Professor Gillespie is funny and interesting.  He really makes you realize how much is out there to experience.  Also makes you wish there was nothing against professor-student relationships.  He's hot and his personality just adds to it.

Gillespie was excellent. He's laugh-out-loud funny, very down to earth and super helpful. He really wants everyone to succeed and pass. You MUST attend lecture AND read the book. About 30% of the material on tests was from the book and the rest from lecture. Everyone should take this class (and be glad there is such a great professor for it!)

I have taken four classes with Prof Gillespie and he is by far my favorite professor at UCLA. He's incredibly easy, but he also makes the material interesting and applicable to learn so you're not just blankly memorizing stuff to later forget. I've taken 5, 111, 113, & 169 and received A's in all of them and I am certainly not an A student.

prof. gillespie is super nice (and, yeah, hot)- in office hours before the midterm and final he'd go over questions that ended up being on the test (like the ones the tas made). his lectures are really intersting & pretty organized. it was an easy class if you put in any effort.

Pretty fair teacher, seems like he knows his stuff but also expects that you do, even though this is an intro class! Anyway gave him a Try and the class was interesting although the project was horrible. Also, almost always has vpl, if you are into that...

analysis.name: Gina Shaffer
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Shaffer is a great teacher!!! I highly recommend taking her.

analysis.name: James Gelvin
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Funny, smart, approachable.  Teaches ME history from a world history viewpoint ("In the 19th century Ottoman sultans looked at France, looked at China, and decided it would be better to a strong power like France than one picked apart like China").  More interested in the big picture than factoids.  Went to class, took notes, and did fine.

He's engaging and really funny; he can be very sarcastic. The TAs for the quarter were also really cool and section is pretty important in understanding the material. A lot of writing, but overall worth it for a really cool class.
One hour p/f seminar on Arab Spring (he hates that phrase, btw).  Took it because I needed the credit, but began looking forward to it every week.  NY sense of humor and he DOES look like House.
The guy already has a class about the Arab uprisings.  Blew me away.  Gives us chapters of a book he is writing on the topic chapter-by-chapter to get our opinions.  OUR opinions!!!

I cannot say enough about how nice this guy is and how beautfifully organized his lectures are.  One should take ANY class he offers.  As for the "bias" comments, unfortunately they are true.  Snide comments,  highlighting certain information while downplaying other info.  It is minor, but it is certainly present.  Still, you've got to take him!

analysis.name: Richard Von Glahn
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The class is discussion based so you really need to show up and talk to get a good grade. No tests, just papers, 3 in total. Lots and lots of reading. It's what discussion and papers are based on so the more of it you do the easier the class is. He really leads you to paper topics and answers in discussion if you pay attention so take notes.

The class is really interesting for those with an interest in Chinese history. The professor explains quite well, and he gives out detailed outlines and even sample exam prompts so that it is easy to prepare for exams.  There are two papers, a midterm and a final. While the papers are graded strictly, the exams are easy indeed.

Tedious class because no one cares about participating in the seminars...but Von Glahn was a good prof. in a discursive way. Overall, he can be tedious because he'd wait 'til someone speaks up after he asked a q. But I'm ok w/that cause i'm the MOST ACTIVE STUDENT IN HIS CLASS! If u hate discussions, then bring a pillow!

analysis.name: Jessica Goldberg
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Goldberg really cares about her students. The reading is tough, but she is more than willing to help you master it. She taught me how to read academic papers with ease, which helped me tremendously in other classes. Just do the work to the best of your ability, and everything will come together

This class and professor is a joke and a waste my time. Considering the circumstances that has came during our quarter, she has nevertheless made it more difficult for not only my peers and I, but also her and the head TA. With 1 out of the 2 TAs at our disposal going on strike, she has decided to up the final material to be a redundant amount.

Professor Goldberg does not care about her students at all. Despite the challenges we faced during this quarter (TA strike, protests) she has not made any modifications to the course, but in fact she made the final more difficult. She basically made the final an AP test worth around 60% of our grade, with 2 timed essays, and a paper.

Professor Goldberg is a great professor who truly cares about her students. The class itself consisted of weekly readings and writing assignments, 1 essay, and a midterm+final. The class was fairly easy considering history requires a lot of memorization. I came in with an A+ before the final and I am south campus major.

analysis.name: Jamie Goodwin White
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Took this class as an upper division elective, after having the class go online and strike through the second half of the quarter. Did not make study guides accessible to anyone who was not in the lecture, made final 1 hour time limit online for 55 questions. Essay prompt was broad with little guidance given by prof. Class needed a TA desperately.

Class was online for the fall quarter. Lectures were tenuous and very difficult to sit through. The class consisted of a midterm, research paper, and a final exam. The communication between the professor and students was lacking entirely. If this class is the only one available in myucla, look again because this is not a class worth taking.

Nice lady, very knowledgable, but a bit ambiguous in her teaching style. To give her credit the subject itself is very fluid and hard to understand, but I had a hard time following her at times. Class was a midterm, 5 page paper and a final. I think I am getting a B. Just do the reading and pay attention and you should be fine.

Very clear and tests are not too tough if you show up to class and skim readings. Gives extra credit on tests which are multiple choice. Would def take again shes lovely. Would recommend to any geography or non-geography major for an easyinteresting upper division.
Jamie was great, but this is pretty difficult material. Wasn't happy with my grade. Expect 30-100 pages of reading per week. The final paper was pretty demanding too.

analysis.name: Gordon Klein
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Teaching is relatively straight forward.  Sort of likes to talk about himself.  Unresponsive and unhelpful outside of class.

Klein is NOT approachable at all. He wud set his OH at 7 and you have to meet like a lot of requirements to be permitted to even email him cuz his personal and professional time is much more important than teaching or helping students. analysis.name: Karen Rowe
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Though a compassionate woman, her lectures are haphazard!! Yabbering at lightning speed, she spews information & expects automatic absorption. With chalk etchings ALL OVER, her writing is disorganized & unreadable. Using outdated references & allusions, she litters students with meaningless handouts. Her tests focus on most minute & random details.

Prof. Rowe is one of the best professors at UCLA.  She's funny, smart, and diligent.  She spends a lot of time making sure her students learn.  The serious English major will love her.  Those who are there to mess around won't do too well.  Her classes are challenging (lots of books, many papers, etc.), but those who work hard will get a lot out of

Professor Rowe was my favorite professor at UCLA. Her class was difficult, but it was only difficult because I had never really put effort into developing my writing skills before taking this course. I will never forget the butter cookies provided during our final exam, nor the hours of help she provided in office hours to make my essays readable.

Oh, please...those who complain about how hard she is or how much work there is in her class (esp. Milton) really don't belong in college! You ARE supposed to work in class, and Prof. Rowe makes you work! She expects a lot but she rewards hard work - you need to be on your game! Those who moan about it - what are you doing at UCLA?

If you want a quarter filled with femi-nazi rhetoric and opinions, stay.  If you want an unbiased class geared towards fostering individual growth DO NOT take this woman's class.  She is arrogant and uncompromising.  She is a great writer and will give you good suggestions on papers, but that does not outweigh the overall experience.

analysis.name: Karen Kevorkian
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best classes that I've taken at UCLA. She is very helpful and gives a ton of feedback on your writing. Learned a lot from her. She can come off as a bit weird at first, and she doesn't like to get intimate with the students, because she says that it makes it harder to grade them, but once you get to know her better, she is very kind.

If you truly want to grow as a creative writer, then Professor Kevorkian's poetry workshop is the one you need to enroll in. She teaches and guides in the fundamentals of poetry. The weekly homework consists of writing one poem, alongside various exercises designed to help strengthen and develop specific poetic devices. She is awesome!

Karen changed my writing in so many amazing ways. I feel like she's the hidden gem of UCLA's creative writing staff. She has a very dry wit and sense of humor and a kinda heart to boot. Take her class and be challenged and intruduced to so many wonderful poets work that are wwriting today. (I swear this isn't paid lol)
